speaker: Alice
content: An odd thing happens when we die. Our senses vanish.
mark: odd adj. 奇怪的，反常的；偶然出现的；奇形怪状的，各种各样的；奇数的；
vanish v. 突然不见，消失；不复存在，灭绝；
senses: Taste, touch, smell, sound, sight 五感:味觉/触觉/嗅觉/听觉/视觉

speaker: Alice
content: Taste, touch, smell, and sound become a distant memory, but our sight -- ah, our sight expands.
mark: distant adj. 遥远的，久远的；冷淡的；远亲的；不同的
expand v. 扩大，增加；扩展，发展；细谈，详述；

speaker: Alice
content: And we can suddenly see the world we've left behind so clearly.
mark: left behind 被遗留

speaker: Alice
content: Of course, most of what's visible to the dead could also be seen by the living, if they only take the time to look.
mark: most of what's visible to the dead 死者能看到的大部分东西 (what 引导的主语从句)
the dead 死者 the living 生者，活着的人们
take the time to 花足够的时间来做某事，做得好或仔细

speaker: Alice
content: Like my friend Gabrielle, I should have seen how unhappy she was, but I didn't.

speaker: Alice
content: I only saw her clothes from Paris, and her platinum jewelry, and her brand-new diamond watch.
mark: platinum n. 铂，白金；adj. 铂的，白金的；
jewelry/jewellery n. 珠宝，首饰
brand-new adj.崭新的，未用过的；最近获得的 brand n.品牌，商标；

speaker: Alice
content: Had I looked closer, I’d have seen that Gabrielle was a drowning woman, desperately in search of a life raft.
mark: Had I looked closer == If I had looked closer 虚拟倒装
I’d have seen == I would have seen 侧重结果的将会发生，口语连读就是I'd've seen
drown v. （声音）压过，盖过；沉浸于，淹没于；（使）淹死
in search of 试图寻找或寻求
raft n. 木排，木筏； 橡皮艇，充气船； life raft 救生筏

speaker: Alice
content: Luckily for her, she found one. Of course, Gabrielle only saw her young gardener as a way to infuse her life with a little excitement.
mark: see sb as a way to do sth 把某人看作是做某事的一种途径 / 方式
infuse v. 使充满（某种感觉），向…灌输；冲泡（茶、药草等），浸渍；输注
infuse ... with ... 用… 注入…；使… 充满…

speaker: Alice
content: But now she was about to discover just how exciting her life could get.
mark: be about to 即将要做某事

speaker: Gabrielle
content: Damn it. Come on! Hurry up! Let's go.
mark: damn 该死，见鬼 严厉批评，谴责；Damn it 该死！

speaker: John
content: Where are the rest of my clothes?

speaker: Gabrielle
content: I'll find them. Just get out. Out the window. ... Hey, honey. You're home early.
mark: You're home early：用于当看到某人已经在家，而且比平常预期的时间要早的时候发出的一种感慨、疑问或者简单陈述
You get home early：描述规律性的行为或具体的动作情况，比如谈论日常的生活作息，或讲述某一天具体到家时间比较早的行为

speaker: Carlos
content: My meeting got canceled. Didn't you take a shower this morning?
mark: cancel => cancelled/canceled v. 取消；宣布作废
My meeting got canceled. 这里的‘get + 过去分词’构成的被动结构，比‘be + 过去分词’的被动更口语化

speaker: Gabrielle
content: I just finished a workout.
mark: workout n. 锻炼，训练；试验；作为锻炼讲时，是可数名词

speaker: Carlos
content: Where's John? That's his truck out front.
mark: truck n. 卡车，载重汽车；手推车

speaker: Alice
content: Gabrielle was panicked. She knew if her husband discovered her secret, she would feel the full force of his wrath.
mark: panicked v. 使恐慌（panic 的过去式和过去分词）= in a panic
full force 全力以赴：以最大的力量、速度或决心进行某项活动或行动。
wrath n. 愤怒，盛怒 full force of his wrath

speaker: John
content: Hey, Mr. Solis.

speaker: Carlos
content: Hey, John, did you take care of that ficus yet?
mark: ficus n. 榕树; 热带榕属植物

speaker: John
content: I'll get to it as soon as I'm done here.
mark: get to 开始，着手; 到达，抵达; 理解，明白
I'll get to it as soon as I'm done here.我在这里的事一做完，就会着手去做它

speaker: Carlos
content: Alright. We need to talk about the hedges later.
mark: hedge n. 树篱；防范措施，防备手段 hedge risk 对冲风险

speaker: Alice
content: But she was quickly reminded that what Carlos couldn't see couldn't hurt her.
mark: she was quickly reminded that ... 在大多数情况下，当that引导宾语从句时，且只起引导作用时是可以省略的。但是，当主句的谓语动词后面的宾语从句比较长，或者从句结构比较复杂，或者为了强调从句的内容等情况时，that最好不要省略。
what Carlos couldn't see couldn't hurt her. 前面的 what Carlos couldn't see 是一个从句，充当主语
